name: Elves Batch
description: Execute one independently shippable Elves batch with validation, review, and checkpointing.

fields.name: continue_after_batch
fields.description: Whether the agent may suggest another batch after this one

steps.name: reread
steps.prompt:
Start the Elves batch by rereading durable memory.

Read `docs/elves/survival-guide.md`, `docs/elves/execution-log.md`, `docs/elves/learnings.md`, `.elves-session.json`, active PR state if present, and the batch goal. Write a short batch plan to `docs/elves/batches/{{ id }}.md`.

steps.name: tag
steps.prompt:
Create a pre-batch checkpoint.

Record current git status, branch, commit, PR status, and intended batch scope in `docs/elves/batches/{{ id }}.md`. If using git tags or commits for checkpoints, create the appropriate lightweight checkpoint.

steps.name: implement
steps.prompt:
Implement exactly this Elves batch: {{ batch_goal }}

Stay inside the batch boundary. Update the execution log as decisions are made. If scope expands, stop and document rather than continuing silently.

steps.name: validate
steps.prompt:
Validate this Elves batch.

Run touched-surface proof first, then the validation commands from the ticket and survival guide. Fix failures only when the fix is within batch scope. Record exact commands and results in `docs/elves/batches/{{ id }}.md`.

on_reject.prompt:
Batch validation was rejected: {{ rejection_reason }}

Repair the batch within scope and rerun validation.

steps.name: review
steps.prompt:
Perform a fresh review of this batch.

Read the diff, execution log, validation evidence, and current PR comments/checks if any. Focus on correctness, regression risk, scope creep, and missing validation. Write findings to `docs/elves/batches/{{ id }}.md`.

on_reject.prompt:
Fresh review requested changes: {{ rejection_reason }}

Fix the review findings and repeat validation.

steps.name: judge
steps.prompt:
Judge whether the batch is safe to checkpoint.

Read any project constitution, invariants, survival guide, diff, tests, and review notes. Emit pass, warn, or fail in `docs/elves/batches/{{ id }}.md`. Fail means return to implementation. Warn means checkpoint but clearly document residual risk.

on_reject.prompt:
Judge verdict failed or was rejected: {{ rejection_reason }}

Address the unsafe condition or stop with a blocker.

steps.name: document
steps.prompt:
Persist durable memory after this batch.

Update `docs/elves/execution-log.md`, `docs/elves/learnings.md`, `docs/elves/survival-guide.md`, and `.elves-session.json` with batch result, validation, decisions, risks, and exact resume instructions.

steps.name: push
steps.prompt:
Checkpoint this Elves batch.

Commit focused changes if appropriate, push the branch when configured, update the PR if one exists, and record commit/push status in the batch log. Do not merge.

steps.name: entropy
steps.prompt:
Decide whether to continue after this Elves batch.

Consider `continue_after_batch`, remaining plan, time budget, validation confidence, review status, and risk. If continuing is safe, create or recommend the next ELVBATCH. If not, stop with exact human next steps.
